help.summary: Sets a **Player** to defend an area around a location. (Only works for AI players)
examples_xml:

    <value name="VALUE-0">
        <block type="EventPlayer"></block>
    </value>
    <value name="VALUE-1">
        <block type="CreateVector">
            <value name="VALUE-0">
                <block type="Number">
                    <field name="NUM">88</field>
                </block>
            </value>
            <value name="VALUE-1">
                <block type="Number">
                    <field name="NUM">45</field>
                </block>
            </value>
            <value name="VALUE-2">
                <block type="Number">
                    <field name="NUM">-30</field>
                </block>
            </value>
        </block>
    </value>
    <value name="VALUE-2">
        <block type="Number">
            <field name="NUM">0</field>
        </block>
    </value>

        <block type="Number">
            <field name="NUM">5</field>

</block>

    <mutation isOnGoingEvent="false"></mutation>
    <field name="NAME">New Rule</field>
    <field name="EVENTTYPE">OnPlayerEarnedKill</field>
    <statement name="CONDITIONS"></statement>
    <statement name="ACTIONS">
        <block type="If">
            <mutation></mutation>
            <value name="VALUE-0">
                <block type="Equals">
                    <value name="VALUE-0">

                            <value name="VALUE-0">
                                <block type="EventPlayer"></block>
                            </value>
                        </block>
                    </value>
                    <value name="VALUE-1">
                        <block type="GetTeam">
                            <value name="VALUE-0">

                                    <field name="NUM">1</field>
                                </block>
                            </value>
                        </block>
                    </value>
                </block>
            </value>
            <statement name="DO">
                <block type="Abort"></block>
            </statement>
        </block>
    </statement>
</block>

ChaseVariableAtRate.name: ChaseVariableAtRate

help.title: ChaseVariableAtRate
help.summary:
Gradually modifies the value of a Variable at a specified rate (value/second) until it reaches the provided limit.
Note: If the limit is changed later, the Variable will continue to be updated from its previous value. To spot modifying the Variable, use StopChasingVariable.
examples_xml:
<block type="ChaseVariableAtRate">
    <value name="VALUE-1">
        <block type="Number">
            <field name="NUM">30</field>
        </block>
    </value>
    <value name="VALUE-2">
        <block type="Number">
            <field name="NUM">1</field>
        </block>
    </value>
</block>

api.signature: mod.ChaseVariableAtRate(variable: Variable, limit: number, deltaPerSecond: number): void

ChaseVariableAtRate.ai_usage_note: Use exact TypeScript signature(s) from api_overloads. Use Help/XML for Blockly behavior.

ChaseVariableOverTime.name: ChaseVariableOverTime

help.title: ChaseVariableOverTime
help.summary:
Gradually modifies the value of a Variable over time (in seconds). The variable's value will reach the limit at the end of the interval.
Note: If the limit is changed later, the Variable will continue to be updated from its previous value. To spot modifying the Variable, use StopChasingVariable.
examples_xml:
<block type="ChaseVariableOverTime">
    <value name="VALUE-1">
        <block type="Number">
            <field name="NUM">100</field>
        </block>
    </value>
    <value name="VALUE-2">
        <block type="Number">
            <field name="NUM">30</field>
        </block>
    </value>
</block>

api.signature: mod.ChaseVariableOverTime(variable: Variable, limit: number, durationSeconds: number): void
api.parameters_raw: variable: Variable, limit: number, durationSeconds: number
api.comment: Gradually modifies the value of a Variable over time (in seconds). The variable's value will reach the limit at the end of the interval.

api_overloads.name: ChaseVariableOverTime
api_overloads.parameters_raw: variable: Variable, limit: number, durationSeconds: number
api_overloads.signature: mod.ChaseVariableOverTime(variable: Variable, limit: number, durationSeconds: number): void

FilteredArray.name: FilteredArray

help.title: FilteredArray
help.summary: Returns a filtered version of the specified Array based on the condition provided. This block cycles through the entire array. You can utilize the CurrentArrayElement block to represent the element in the Array for each iteration. For an Array like AllPlayers, CurrentArrayElement would represent each Player in that Array. You can then build your filter condition based on a property of that Player (like score, or some custom player Variable value).
examples_xml:
<block type="FilteredArray">
    <value name="VALUE-0">
        <block type="AllPlayers" />
    </value>
    <value name="VALUE-1">
        <block type="Equals">
            <value name="VALUE-0">
                <block type="GetTeam">
                    <value name="VALUE-0">
                        <block type="CurrentArrayElement" />
                    </value>
                </block>
            </value>
            <value name="VALUE-1">
                <block type="GetTeam">
                    <value name="VALUE-0">
                        <block type="Number">
                            <field name="NUM">3</field>
                        </block>
                    </value>
                </block>
            </value>
        </block>
    </value>
</block>

FilteredArray.ai_usage_note: Help/Blockly entry only; no direct mod.* API signature found by robust parser.

            <value name="CONDITION">
                <block type= "Equals">

                    </value>
                    <value name= "VALUE-1">
                        <block type= "GolmudTrainStopReasonItem">

                        </block>
                    </value>
                </block>
            </value>                   
        </block>
    </statement>    
    <statement name= "ACTIONS">
        <block type= "GolmudTrainSendMoveCommand">
            <value name= "VALUE-0">
                <block type= "GolmudTrainMoveCommandsItem">
                    <field name= "VALUE-0">GolmudTrainMoveCommands</field>
                    <field name= "VALUE-1">MoveWest</field>
                </block>                
            </value>                   
        </block>
    </statement>
</block>

If.name: If

help.title: If
help.summary: A special block which evaluates conditions to control the flow of Actions in the If, Else If, and Else branches.
examples_xml:
<block type="If">
    <mutation elseif="1" else="1"></mutation>
    <value name="VALUE-0">
        <block type="Equals">
            <value name="VALUE-0">
                <block type="GetTeam">
                    <value name="VALUE-0">
                        <block type="EventPlayer"></block>
                    </value>
                </block>
            </value>
            <value name="VALUE-1">
                <block type="GetTeam">

Or.name: Or

help.title: Or
help.summary: %{help.or.summary}
examples_xml:
<block type="Or">
    <value name="VALUE-0">
        <block type="Equals">
            <value name="VALUE-0">
                <block type="GetPlayerKills">
                    <value name="VALUE-0">
                        <block type="EventPlayer" />
                    </value>
                </block>
            </value>
            <value name="VALUE-1">
                <block type="Number">
                    <field name="NUM">20</field>
                </block>
            </value>
        </block>
    </value>
    <value name="VALUE-1">
        <block type="LessThanEqualTo">
            <value name="VALUE-0">
                <block type="GetMatchTimeRemaining" />
            </value>
            <value name="VALUE-1">
                <block type="Number">
                    <field name="NUM">0</field>
                </block>
            </value>
        </block>
    </value>
</block>

api.comment: Returns a boolean based on whether either of the two inputs are true.

                        <block type="Number">

                        <block type="Number">

                    <value name="VALUE-0">
                        <block type="Number">
                            <field name="NUM">0</field>
                        </block>
                    </value>
                    <value name="VALUE-1">
                        <block type="Number">
                            <field name="NUM">0</field>
                        </block>
                    </value>
                    <value name="VALUE-2">
                        <block type="Number">
                            <field name="NUM">0</field>
                        </block>

            <field name="NUM">10</field>

SendPortalLogToAdmin.name: SendPortalLogToAdmin

help.title: SendPortalLogToAdmin
help.summary: Sends Portal Log to the admin client of the current session when hosting via "Host" (Dedicated Server). For "Host Locally," Portal Log is always available locally, so this will do nothing. Writes to PortalLog.txt on the admin's client. If admin doesn't exist, this will do nothing. Quota applies for valid log sends per session.
examples_xml:
<block type="SendPortalLogToAdmin">
</block>

api.comment: Sends Portal Log to the admin client of the current session when hosting via "Host" (Dedicated Server). For "Host Locally," Portal Log is always available locally, so this will do nothing. Writes to PortalLog.txt on the admin's client. If admin doesn't exist, this will do nothing. Quota applies for valid log sends per session.

    <value name="VALUE-0">
        <block type="GetVariable"></block>

        <block type="CreateTransform">
            <value name="VALUE-0">
                <block type="CreateVector">
                    <value name="VALUE-0">
                        <block type="Number">
                            <field name="NUM">80</field>
                        </block>
                    </value>
                    <value name="VALUE-1">
                        <block type="Number">
                            <field name="NUM">15</field>
                        </block>
                    </value>
                    <value name="VALUE-2">
                        <block type="Number">
                            <field name="NUM">40</field>
                        </block>
                    </value>
                </block>
            </value>
            <value name="VALUE-1">
                <block type="UpVector"/>
            </value>            
        </block>
    </value>
    <value name="VALUE-2">
        <block type="Number">
            <field name="NUM">60</field>
        </block>
    </value>    
    <value name="VALUE-3">
        <block type="Boolean">
            <field name="BOOL">TRUE</field>
        </block>
    </value>
    <value name="VALUE-4">
        <block type="Boolean">
            <field name="BOOL">TRUE</field>
        </block>

help.title: SetSpectatingFiltersForAll
help.summary: Sets the spectating filters. SpectatingGroup sets the selectable players in the spectating UI. ownSquadOnly and ownTeamOnly limit whether a player can spectate other squads/teams after currently spectated one is eliminated

SortedArray.name: SortedArray

help.title: SortedArray
help.summary: Returns a sorted version of the specified Array given a Number value to sort by (ascending). CurrentArrayElement can be utilized to represent each value in the Array. In the following example, CurrentArrayElement is used to represent each Player in AllPlayers. GetGameModeScore is used with CurrentArrayElement to return the score, used as a rank, to sort the Array in ascending order.
examples_xml:
<block type= "SortedArray">
    <value name= "VALUE-0">
        <block type= "AllPlayers"/>
    </value>
    <value name= "VALUE-1">
        <block type= "GetGameModeScore">
            <value name= "VALUE-0">
                <block type= "CurrentArrayElement"/>
            </value>
        </block>
    </value>
</block>

api.comment: Returns a sorted version of the specified Array given a Number value to sort by (ascending). CurrentArrayElement can be utilized to represent each value in the Array. In the following example, CurrentArrayElement is used to represent each Player in AllPlayers. GetGameModeScore is used with CurrentArrayElement to return the score, used as a rank, to sort the Array in ascending order.

        <block type="conditionBlock">
            <value name="CONDITION">
                <block type="Equals">
                    <value name="VALUE-0">

                            <value name="VALUE-0">
                                <block type="EventPlayer"></block>
                            </value>

                    </value>

                        <block type="GetTeam">
                            <value name="VALUE-0">

                        </block>
                    </value>

            </value>
        </block>

    <statement name="ACTIONS">
        <block type="SetGameModeScore">
            <value name="VALUE-0">
                <block type="EventPlayer"></block>
            </value>
            <value name="VALUE-1">
                <block type="Add">
                    <value name="VALUE-0">
                        <block type="GetGameModeScore">
                            <value name="VALUE-0">
                                <block type="EventPlayer"></block>
                            </value>
                        </block>
                    </value>
                    <value name="VALUE-1">
                        <block type="Number">
                            <field name="NUM">1</field>
                        </block>
                    </value>
                </block>
            </value>
        </block>
    </statement>
</block>
